title: Stow police file criminal complaint against officer
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Detective Jason Rogers improperly accessed secure databases to find personal information about someone he knows, officials said.
The Stow Police Department recently filed a criminal complaint against one of its officers for improperly accessing databases of personal information.
The allegations concern Detective Jason Rogers, officials said in a statement.

title: Patriots 2019 draft pick Joejuan Williams returns for coaching fellowship
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots "Back home," Williams wrote.
Joejuan Williams plays during a preseason game.
Paul Sancya / AP Photo Former Patriots cornerback Joejuan Williams has been selected as a member of the Bill Walsh Diversity Coaching fellowship.

title: Was the World Cup worth it? Early data suggest Boston’s bet is paying off.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: World Cup Boston bars saw a 28% jump in transactions, Airbnb revenue climbed 27%, hotel rates surged, and the MBTA set ridership records during the FIFA World Cup.
Scottish fans fill the street in front of Hennessy’s bar next to Faneuil Hall.
(John Tlumacki/Boston Globe) By Annie Jonas July 20, 2026 | 10:14 AM 5 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: REI to close its longtime Boston store next month
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The Landmark Center location, which has operated for 23 years, will close on Aug.
13 as the outdoor retailer continues to struggle.
Shoppers inside REI's Boston location.

title: Review & setlist: Saturday’s Garden show proved that what the world needs now is Al, Weird Al
author: Perron Eaton
bullets: Concert Reviews "Weird Al" Yankovic's performance for a sold-out TD Garden crowd was weird, and it was great.
“Weird Al” Yankovic, right, plays the accordion alongside guitarist Jim West last year at the Wang Theatre.
Yankovic upsized his show this past weekend, playing to a full TD Garden.

title: These are the beaches closed in Mass. this week
author: Peter Chianca and Heather Alterisio
bullets: Environment About 30 beaches remained unfit for swimming as of Monday morning.
Walden Pond is open for swimming, but about 30 other beaches have been deemed unsafe this week.

Lucy Lu/The New York Times As a big vacation and staycation week kicks off in Massachusetts, about 30 beaches remained closed around the Bay State, according to the state’s interactive beach water quality dashboard.

title: Looking to buy in New England? Vacancy rates may paint a misleading housing picture.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Home Buying A new LendingTree report finds that many vacant homes in tourism-heavy New England states are seasonal properties, leaving fewer options for year-round residents.
The ski slopes of Mt.
Snow are seen behind some vacation rental properties to the west of the town of Dover, Vermont, on April 25, 2019.

title: Lindsay Clancy murder trial: Jury selection begins Monday. Here’s a quick primer on the case.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Lindsay Clancy is about to stand trial in the 2023 deaths of her three children.

Lindsay Clancy, with her attorney, Kevin Reddington, appears in Plymouth Superior Court for a pretrial conference, Thursday, June 18, 2026.

title: Boston police officer facing charges after allegedly assaulting family member
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Mark J.
Loewen, 50, of Roslindale, faces a felony vandalism charge along with two counts of misdemeanor assault and battery, court records show.
